policy_structure.evaluation: 5-score pipeline: model score, CLV trust, real edge, confidence, domain analysis
policy_structure.qualification: score >= minimumScore AND within board caps → qualified
policy_structure.priority_order: First qualified target in priority order wins

code_locations: intelligence-readiness.test.ts — policy validation (perSlate, perSport, perGame caps tested)

proofs.notes: Promotion rules are defined in promotion-service.ts with a 5-score evaluation pipeline. 2 distinct promotion targets used. Each target has explicit minimumScore and boardCaps policy. Qualification is deterministic.

cap_enforcement.test: TC4: board size cap limits board to BOARD_SIZE_CAP rows
cap_enforcement.per_target: Each promotion target has independent boardCaps policy

proofs.notes: Board caps enforced via BOARD_SIZE_CAP constant and per-target boardCaps (perSlate/perSport/perGame). 200 board rows, max rank 18. Test TC4 validates cap enforcement. Policy validation tests assert all caps positive.

dedup_mechanisms: idempotencyKey on outbox enqueue prevents duplicate rows
enqueueDistributionAtomic uses Postgres transaction for atomicity
promotion_target set once — no re-promotion path without force_promote

proofs.notes: Zero duplicate pick+target combinations in 62 outbox rows. Dedup enforced via idempotencyKey + atomic enqueue + single-write promotion_target.

proofs.control: Promotion decisions are stored and auditable

audit_schema.payload_includes: Full decision context: scores, policy, target, qualification reason

code_locations: promotion-service.ts:269 — audit on primary promotion decision

proofs.notes: 100 promotion audit entries in audit_log. Actions: promotion.suppressed, promotion.suppress, promotion.force_promote. Every promotion decision (qualified/suppressed/force/rollback) produces an audit record with full context.
